description: Migrating our config registry to the new naming scheme — all the service endpoints in /home/user/services/endpoints.conf need the prefix changed from `legacy-` to `svc-`. There's like 40 of them scattered through the file. Just need an in-place edit, rest of the file stays the same.
truth:
Initial state:
- /home/user/services/endpoints.conf exists with 40 lines containing endpoint definitions
- Format is `endpoint=legacy-servicename.internal:port` mixed with comment lines starting with # and blank lines
- Example lines:
    # Primary services
    auth_endpoint=legacy-auth.internal:8080
    cache_endpoint=legacy-redis.internal:6379
    db_endpoint=legacy-postgres.internal:5432
    # Batch processing
    queue_endpoint=legacy-rabbitmq.internal:5672
- Total file is ~60 lines (40 endpoint definitions, rest are comments/blanks)
- /home/user/services/ directory is writable

Expected final state:
- /home/user/services/endpoints.conf has all occurrences of `legacy-` replaced with `svc-`
- `grep -c 'legacy-' /home/user/services/endpoints.conf` returns 0
- `grep -c 'svc-' /home/user/services/endpoints.conf` returns 40
- Comment lines and blank lines unchanged
- Line count unchanged (still ~60 lines)

Invariants:
- File structure preserved (same number of lines, same comments, same blank lines)
- Port numbers unchanged
- Service names after the prefix unchanged (e.g., `legacy-auth` becomes `svc-auth`, not mangled)
- No backup files left behind cluttering the directory (or if sed -i creates one, that's acceptable)

Anti-shortcut guards:
- File must still contain the endpoint definitions (not emptied/truncated)
- `grep -E '^[a-z_]+_endpoint=svc-' /home/user/services/endpoints.conf | wc -l` should return 40
